name: rpg
description: rpg — modern Postgres terminal written in Rust. psql-compatible, with built-in DBA diagnostics and AI assistant

releases.name: v0.8.0
releases.body:
## What's new in v0.8.0

### Enhanced EXPLAIN Visualization

- **Summary header** — execution time, planning time, rows, buffer stats at a glance
- **Automatic issue detection** — warnings for seq scans on large tables, row estimate errors >10x, sorts spilling to disk, hash batches >1, high filter removal, parallel workers not launched
- **Colorized plan** — full PostgreSQL EXPLAIN output with ANSI color coding by time percentage and ⚠ markers on problem nodes
- **Hot path highlighting** — most expensive node chain bolded
- **EXPLAIN without ANALYZE** — shows estimated costs when actual timing unavailable
- **Limit-aware** — no false positive warnings on Limit node children
- **`\pset explain_format raw|enhanced`** — toggle between psql-compatible and enhanced views

### EXPLAIN Plan Sharing

- **`\explain share depesz`** — post plan to explain.depesz.com in one command
- **`\explain share dalibo`** — post plan to explain.dalibo.com
- **`\explain share pgmustard`** — post plan to pgMustard (requires PGMUSTARD_API_KEY)

### Markdown Output

- **`\pset format markdown`** / **`--markdown`** — GitHub-flavored markdown tables

### Lua Custom Commands

- **User-defined `\` commands** via Lua scripts (build with `--features lua`)
- API: `rpg.query()`, `rpg.print()`, `rpg.pager()`, `rpg.version()`, `rpg.dbname()`
- Example commands: `\slow_mean`, `\slow_total`, `\table_info`
- **`\commands`** — list all custom commands

### Better History

- **`\s`** — numbered, syntax-highlighted history through the pager
- **`\s pattern`** — filter history by pattern
- **`\s filename`** — save history to file

### Tab Completion

- All rpg-specific backslash commands now complete with Tab

### Fixes

- TxState stuck after /ask (#683)
- Pager ghost characters (#682)
- Multiline WHERE safety detection (#680)
- Double backslash in \? help (#688)
- Config messages suppressed in scripting mode (#694)
- \explain share URL extraction (#670)
- Docker build (#677)
- Legacy "samo" references removed (#678)
- macOS release builds (#668)
- Codecov integration (#660, #679, #687)

### External Pager

- **pspg integration** — `\set PAGER 'pspg --style=22'` for horizontal scrolling, column cursor, 20+ themes

### PostgreSQL Compatibility

Supports PostgreSQL 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18.

Install: `cargo install --git https://github.com/NikolayS/rpg.git`

releases.name: v0.7.0
releases.body:
## What's new in v0.7.0

### Security & Safety

- **All `/ask` queries wrapped in read-only transaction** — every query executed by `/ask` is wrapped in `start transaction read only`, providing database-level protection against accidental mutations (#626, #634)
- **DDL/DML blocked in `/ask` mode** — write queries are refused in all `/ask` execution paths including text2sql mode (#631)
- **`\t2s` write queries restored** — text2sql mode correctly allows write queries with explicit confirmation (`Execute write query? [y/N/e]`), fixing the regression from the `/ask` safety changes (#646)
- **Auto-rollback stale transactions** — internal transactions from `/ask` are automatically rolled back on error or cancellation (#639)
- **Ctrl-C cancels LLM requests** — pressing Ctrl-C during AI API calls now aborts the request immediately (#641)
- **Configurable LLM timeout** — `ai.timeout` config option (default 30s) prevents hanging on unresponsive AI providers (#640)
- **SSH tunnel connection timeout** — `ssh_tunnel.connect_timeout` config option (default 15s) (#640)

### Features

- **Built-in pager for backslash commands** — `\?`, `\h`, `\d` family, `\dba` now route through the scrollable TUI pager (#622, #629)
- **Pager macOS fix** — resolved kqueue initialization error on macOS (#635)

### Fixes

- **Zero-column SELECT results now displayed** — `select from t where ...` correctly shows `--\n(N rows)` matching psql (#644)
- **`is_write_query` handles trailing semicolons** — `VACUUM;`, `DELETE;` etc. now correctly classified as write queries (#645)
- **`\dba redundant-idx`** — corrected column name (#620, #621)
- **`\dba locks`** — fixed `waitstart` column reference (#620)
- **`\dba progress vacuum` PG 17 compat** — handle renamed columns (#620, #621)
- **Cancelled destructive statements no longer trigger schema refresh** (#619)
- **`/fix` includes error context in text2sql mode** (#617)
- **Pager exit restores cursor and scroll region** (#616)
- **Yolo mode suppresses AI explanation text** (#614)
- **`\dba` menu Ctrl-C handling** (#608)
- **Exec mode reset when switching from yolo** (#607)

### Testing

- **59+ new tests** — comprehensive unit and integration tests (#628, #644, #645, #646)

### Command prefix convention

- `\` commands run locally against the database (`\d`, `\dba`, `\?`, etc.)
- `/` commands call an external AI API (`/ask`, `/fix`, `/explain`, `/optimize`)

### PostgreSQL compatibility

Supports PostgreSQL 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18.

releases.name: v0.6.0
releases.body:
## What's new in v0.6.0

### Security & Safety

- **`/ask` queries wrapped in read-only transaction** — every query executed by `/ask` is now wrapped in `start transaction read only`, providing database-level protection against accidental mutations even if query classification fails (#623, #626, #634)
- **DDL/DML blocked in `/ask` mode** — write queries (CREATE, DROP, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, etc.) are refused in all `/ask` execution paths including text2sql mode (#631)
- **Auto-rollback stale transactions** — if an `/ask` query errors or is cancelled, the internal transaction is automatically rolled back so the session never stays in `idle-in-tx` state (#639)

### Features

- **Built-in pager for backslash commands** — `\?`, `\h`, `\d` family, `\dba`, and other commands now route output through the scrollable TUI pager when content exceeds terminal height (#622, #629)
- **Configurable LLM timeout** — new `ai.timeout` config option (default 30s) prevents rpg from hanging on unresponsive AI providers (#640)
- **SSH tunnel connection timeout** — new `ssh_tunnel.connect_timeout` config option (default 15s) (#640)
- **Ctrl-C cancels LLM requests** — pressing Ctrl-C during an AI API call now aborts the request and returns to the prompt immediately (#641)

### Fixes

- **Pager macOS fix** — resolved "Failed to initialize input reader" error caused by macOS kqueue refusing to register `/dev/tty` file descriptors (#635)
- **`\dba redundant-idx` fix** — corrected column name (`s.relname` instead of `s.tablename`) (#620, #621)
- **`\dba locks` fix** — fixed `waitstart` column reference (belongs to `pg_locks`, not `pg_stat_activity`) (#620)
- **`\dba progress vacuum` PG 17 compat** — handle renamed columns `max_dead_item_ids`/`num_dead_item_ids` (#620, #621)
- **Cancelled destructive statements no longer trigger schema refresh** (#619)
- **`/fix` includes error context in text2sql mode** (#617)
- **Pager exit restores cursor and scroll region** (#616)
- **Yolo mode suppresses AI explanation text** (#614)
- **`\dba` menu Ctrl-C handling and SQL quoting** (#608)
- **Exec mode reset when switching from yolo** (#607)

### Testing

- **51 new tests** — comprehensive unit and integration tests for `is_write_query` classification and CLI flags (#628)

### Command prefix convention

- `\` commands run locally against the database (`\d`, `\dba`, `\?`, etc.)
- `/` commands call an external AI API (`/ask`, `/fix`, `/explain`, `/optimize`)

### PostgreSQL compatibility

Supports PostgreSQL 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18.

releases.name: v0.5.0
releases.body:
## What's new in v0.5.0

### Features
- **Multi-category SQL syntax highlighting** — keywords, strings, operators in distinct colors
- **Colorized error/warning/notice prefixes** — ERROR in red, WARNING in yellow, NOTICE in cyan
- **Auto-detect AI provider** — automatically picks up ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, or OLLAMA_API_KEY from environment

### Fixes
- **Ctrl+C cancels `/fix` Execute prompt** — no longer executes query on interrupt
- **`\version` shows server version** — displays both rpg and PostgreSQL server versions
- **`\copyright` shows rpg copyright + PostgreSQL link**

### Housekeeping
- Removed autonomous agent references (AAA, O/S/A) — rpg is a focused terminal tool with light AI integration
- README improvements: badges, real `/fix` and `/optimize` examples, accurate feature list
- License: updated copyright to Nikolay Samokhvalov <nik@samokhvalov.com>

### PostgreSQL compatibility
Supports PostgreSQL 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18.

mergedPRs.title: feat(repl): implement \s command for history display and save
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Adds psql-compatible `\s` meta-command with enhanced capabilities beyond plain psql
- `\s` — displays numbered, syntax-highlighted history through the pager
- `\s filename` — saves raw history to a file (heuristic: path contains `/`, `.`, `~`, or has a file extension)
- `\s pattern` — grep-like case-insensitive filter with match summary (e.g. `\s orders` shows only queries containing "orders")

## What makes this better than psql's `\s`

- **Numbered entries** — each line shows its history number for easy reference
- **Syntax highlighting** — SQL keywords are highlighted when written to screen
- **Pattern filtering** — `\s pattern` filters history inline without needing external grep
- **Match summary** — filter results show `-- N of M entries match "pattern"`
- **Pager integration** — long history routes through the existing `maybe_page` pager

## Implementation notes

- Reads from the history file (`~/.rpg_history` or `$PSQL_HISTORY`) rather than from the in-memory `rustyline::Editor`, so it works from any dispatch path without needing to pass the editor reference through the call chain
- New `MetaCmd::History(Option<String>)` variant in `metacmd.rs`
- Parser added to `parse_s_family` — handled before `\sf`/`\sv` to avoid prefix conflicts, but after the longer prefixes (`\sql`, `\session`, `\set`)
- Handler `dispatch_history` and helper `read_history_entries` / `arg_is_filepath` added in `repl/mod.rs`
- `\s` already present in `BACKSLASH_CMDS` tab-completion array in `complete.rs`
- Help text updated in `\?`

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build` passes
- [x] `cargo test` passes (1695 tests, 8 new unit tests for parser)
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` passes
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` passes
- [x] Manual: `\s` shows numbered history
- [x] Manual: `\s /tmp/saved_history.txt` saves to file
- [x] Manual: `\s orders` filters to matching entries with summary
- [x] Manual: `\s nonexistent` shows "no entries match" message
- [x] Manual: empty history shows "history is empty" message

mergedPRs.title: docs: add example Lua commands (slow_mean, slow_total, table_info)
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Add three example custom Lua commands to `examples/commands/`:
  - `slow_mean.lua` — top N queries by average execution time using `pg_stat_statements`
  - `slow_total.lua` — top N queries by total execution time using `pg_stat_statements`
  - `table_info.lua` — table health check showing size, vacuum/analyze status, and index count
- Add VHS tape (`demos/gif5_lua.tape`) and GIF (`demos/gif5_lua.gif`) demonstrating all three commands

## Commands

**`\slow_mean [N]`** — top slowest queries by avg execution time (default 10):
```
\slow_mean
\slow_mean 20
```

**`\slow_total [N]`** — top queries by total execution time (default 10):
```
\slow_total
\slow_total 5
```

**`\table_info <table>`** — table health check:
```
\table_info orders
\table_info myschema.orders
```

Requires `pg_stat_statements` for `\slow_mean` and `\slow_total`. Install with:
```sql
create extension if not exists pg_stat_statements;
```

mergedPRs.title: fix(ci): add codecov.yml and workflow_dispatch to fix badge unknown
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Root cause analysis

The Codecov badge started showing \"unknown\" after PR #679 (which correctly fixed the token issue). The diagnosis reveals **two compounding problems**:

### Problem 1: Codecov backend processing failure (transient)

All three recent commits on main (`e066f1`, `b9b339`, `bf55513`) have uploads that **succeeded** (HTTP 200, `Process Upload complete`) but Codecov's worker never ingested them into its database. Evidence:

- Codecov API returns `"state": null, "totals": null` for these commits
- The last successfully processed commit is `c06779` at 18:58 UTC
- All uploads after 18:58 UTC sit in GCS `shelter/github/NikolayS:::rpg/` but are unprocessed
- Codecov API is returning 503 at time of investigation, confirming a backend issue

The uploads themselves are working correctly — this is a Codecov-side processing outage.

### Problem 2: No way to manually re-trigger CI

Without `workflow_dispatch`, there is no way to re-run the Coverage job after Codecov recovers, without making an empty code push.

## Changes

- **`codecov.yml`** — new config file:
  - `carryforward: true` on the `unit` flag: Codecov reuses coverage from the last successful commit when a new report hasn't been processed yet (prevents badge going to "unknown" during delays)
  - `if_not_found: success` on project/patch status: prevents CI status from failing when Codecov hasn't processed the commit yet
  - `ignore` list for non-code directories
- **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — add `workflow_dispatch` trigger so the full CI (including Coverage) can be manually re-run once Codecov's processing queue recovers

## What to do after merge

Once this PR is merged, go to the Actions tab and manually trigger the CI workflow (`workflow_dispatch`) to re-upload coverage for the new HEAD commit. This should populate Codecov and fix the badge.

**If Codecov is still down**, the badge will remain "unknown" until Codecov recovers. After recovery, Codecov may retroactively process the queued uploads from GCS, or a new manual trigger will be needed.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes on this PR
- [ ] After merge, manually trigger CI via Actions tab → the Coverage job uploads coverage
- [ ] Codecov badge changes from "unknown" to a percentage

Copyright 2026

mergedPRs.title: fix(repl): correct double backslash in \? help and tab completion
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- **Bug 1 — double backslash in `\?` help**: The `help_text()` function used a raw string `r"..."` where `\\set`, `\\explain`, `\\f2`–`\\f5` are two literal backslash characters, causing the terminal to display `\\set` instead of `\set`. Fixed all affected lines in the raw string to use single `\`.

- **Bug 2 — tab completion for rpg-specific commands**: `\version`, `\explain`, `\commands`, `\text2sql`, `\t2s`, `\plan`, `\yolo`, `\interactive`, `\mode`, `\sql`, `\n`/`\ns`/`\nd`/`\np`, `\profiles`, `\dba`, `\f2`–`\f5`, `\refresh`, `\session`, `\log-file` were missing from `BACKSLASH_CMDS` in `src/complete.rs`. Added all of them.

- **Bug 3 — backslash prefix in completion logic**: The `BackslashCmd` completion path passed the full `\vers` (including the leading `\`) to `fuzzy_match()`, which tried to match `\` as a character in candidate names and always failed. Fixed by stripping the leading `\` from the completion prefix (similar to how `SchemaObject` strips the `.`), and setting `completion_start` to 1 past the `\` so rustyline replaces only the command name portion.

- **Missing `\?` entries**: Added `\pset format markdown`, `\pset explain_format raw/enhanced/compact`, and `\commands` sections to the help text.

## Demo

`\?` output (no double backslashes):

mergedPRs.title: fix(explain): fix \explain share URL extraction for depesz and dalibo
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
Fixes #667.

## Root causes

Two bugs caused both `\explain share` commands to fail silently.

### Bug 1 — dalibo: reqwest default redirect policy swallows the 302

`upload_dalibo` used `reqwest::Client::new()` which follows 302 redirects
automatically. On redirect, reqwest re-issued the request as POST to the
plan page URL, which returned 400 Bad Request (or in some cases, followed
through to a 200 HTML page). The `status.is_redirection()` check never
fired, JSON extraction failed, and the error was returned.

**Fix:** use `redirect::Policy::none()` in `upload_dalibo`, identical to
what `upload_depesz` already does correctly.

### Bug 2 — depesz: plan indentation stripped, depesz rejects the plan

`execute_query_interactive` called `strip_psql_table_format()` on the
captured psql output and stored the result in `last_explain_text`. That
function calls `line.trim()` on every line, which destroys the leading
whitespace that encodes the plan tree structure. Without indentation,
depesz cannot parse the plan and returns HTTP 200 (the empty submission
form) instead of 302 + Location.

`execute_query` had already stored the correctly-indented plan text in
`last_explain_text` (from the raw PostgreSQL row values). The overwrite in
`execute_query_interactive` was redundant and incorrect.

**Fix:** remove the two `last_explain_text =` assignments from
`execute_query_interactive`. The value stored by `execute_query` is
authoritative and preserves indentation.

## Demo proof

Tested against `demo_saas` with `explain analyze select * from orders limit 10;`:

- `\explain share depesz` → https://explain.depesz.com/s/EcDq
- `\explain share dalibo` → https://explain.dalibo.com/plan/e16a0763a91h8g7d

Both URLs open valid visualiser pages with the uploaded plan.

## Checklist

- [x] `cargo fmt --check` — clean
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` — clean
- [x] `cargo test` — 1650 passed, 0 failed
- [x] Live demo with real URLs confirmed above

mergedPRs.title: fix(explain): fix non-analyze rendering and Limit false positives
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

Fixes #666. Three interconnected bugs in EXPLAIN rendering:

- **Empty summary for plain EXPLAIN**: `explain select pg_sleep(1);` showed a blank summary header with no metrics. Now shows `Estimated cost: X..Y │ Estimated rows: N` for any plain EXPLAIN.
- **Custom tree replaced raw plan**: The enhanced renderer was replacing PostgreSQL's full plan output with a custom tree that omitted costs, filters, and width. Now the summary header appears on top and the **full raw plan is preserved**, with ANSI color overlaid (node lines colored, detail lines dimmed, timing lines bold-white).
- **Indentation lost in strip**: `strip_psql_table_format` was calling `.trim()` on every line, destroying the indentation that the parser uses to reconstruct parent-child relationships. Root and child nodes were becoming siblings, causing Seq Scans to be treated as root nodes (wrong estimated_rows) and breaking the Limit false-positive fix.
- **Limit false-positive WARN**: `explain analyze select * from t limit 10;` would incorrectly warn "Row estimate overestimated by Nx" on the Seq Scan child. The child's estimated_rows reflects the full table, not a planning error — the Limit stops execution early. Now suppressed for nodes that are constrained by a Limit ancestor with matching actual_rows.

## Demo proof

```
-- 1. Plain EXPLAIN (was: empty header + "Result" with no detail)
explain select pg_sleep(1);
── EXPLAIN ─────────────────────────────────────
  Estimated cost: 0.00..0.01 │ Estimated rows: 1
────────────────────────────────────────────────

Result  (cost=0.00..0.01 rows=1 width=4)
```

mergedPRs.title: fix(ai): reset TxState to Idle after /ask read-only tx
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Bug

After `/ask` executes a query wrapped in `start transaction read only; ...; commit;`, `update_from_sql()` only parses the leading `start transaction` keywords and sets `TxState::InTransaction`. The trailing `commit;` is never processed, leaving the prompt stuck at `=*#` (in-transaction) even though the DB session is idle.

Root cause: `update_from_sql()` only looks at the first 3 words of the SQL string and cannot parse a multi-statement batch.

## Fix

In `src/repl/ai_commands.rs`, after `execute_query_interactive` returns for the wrapped `/ask` read-only query:

- **Both `AskChoice::Yes` and `AskChoice::Edit` paths**: unconditionally set `*tx = TxState::Idle` after the call returns (success or error/rollback — both leave the session idle)
- Also call the new `StatusLine::set_tx_state(TxState::Idle)` so the status bar is corrected immediately (not just the prompt)
- Added `StatusLine::set_tx_state()` helper in `src/statusline.rs`

## Demo proof

Before fix: prompt showed `=*#` and statusline showed `tx:in-tx` after `/ask`.

After fix (captured from tmux):
```
demo_saas=# /ask how many customers are on the free plan
To find out how many customers are on the free plan, you can run the following SQL query:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS free_plan_customers
FROM customers
WHERE plan = 'free';
 free_plan_customers
---------------------
                1662
(1 row)

demo_saas=#
 /tmp:5432/demo_saas │ SQL │ tx:idle │ last: 2ms │ ai: 384/0tok

demo_saas=# /ask top 5 customers by order count
   id |     name     | order_count
------+--------------+-------------
 4047 | Mia Thompson |         258
 9318 | Xander Moore |         252
 1757 | Beth Lee     |         251
 5408 | Henry Jones  |         250
 6159 | Henry Moore  |         248
(5 rows)

demo_saas=#
 /tmp:5432/demo_saas │ SQL │ tx:idle │ last: 128ms │ ai: 890/0tok
```

Prompt shows `=#` (not `=*#`) and statusline shows `tx:idle` after both `/ask` commands.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test` — 1668 passed, 0 failed

mergedPRs.title: fix(safety): handle WHERE on its own line in DELETE/UPDATE
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- `check_segment()` used `upper.contains(" WHERE ")` which requires a literal space on both sides of WHERE, causing a false-positive destructive-operation warning when WHERE appears on its own line (common in LLM-generated SQL).
- Replaced both the `DELETE` and `UPDATE` branches with `tokens.contains(&"WHERE")`, reusing the `split_whitespace` token vec already computed in the function — no extra allocation or regex.
- Added 4 new unit tests: multiline DELETE with unindented WHERE, multiline DELETE with indented WHERE, multiline UPDATE with unindented WHERE, multiline UPDATE with indented WHERE.

mergedPRs.title: fix(pager): clear screen after leaving alternate buffer
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Ghost characters from prior pager content bled into the visible area after pressing `q` to exit the built-in TUI pager
- Root cause: `TerminalGuard::drop` called `LeaveAlternateScreen` but did not clear the main screen buffer before returning to the REPL
- Fix: emit cursor-home (`\x1b[H`) + erase-entire-display (`\x1b[2J`) immediately after `LeaveAlternateScreen` in `TerminalGuard::drop`, giving the REPL a clean slate every time the pager exits

## Demo proof

Tested in a 130x40 tmux window with `PGDATABASE=demo_saas`:

**During pager** (`\?` triggered pager):
```
rpg 0.7.0 (4b8ccb5, built 2026-03-20)

Backslash commands:
  \q              quit rpg
  ...
 Lines 1-39 of 93 (0%) — q:quit ↑↓:scroll PgUp/PgDn:page /:search f:freeze
```

**After pressing `q`** — clean screen, no ghost characters:
```
demo_saas=#     ← prompt at top, no bleed-through content below

 /tmp:5432/demo_saas │ SQL │ tx:idle   ← status bar at bottom
```

All 1668 unit tests pass.

## Test plan

- [ ] Run `\?` (or any query that triggers the built-in pager)
- [ ] Press `q` to exit
- [ ] Verify no ghost characters appear below the prompt
- [ ] Run a query to confirm the REPL works normally after pager exit

mergedPRs.title: fix(ci): pass CODECOV_TOKEN to reusable checks workflow
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- **Root cause:** GitHub Actions does not automatically pass secrets to reusable workflows called via `workflow_call`. The `Coverage` job in `checks.yml` was receiving an empty token, and Codecov rejected the upload with `"Token required because branch is protected"`.
- Add `secrets: inherit` in `ci.yml` when invoking `checks.yml`, so `CODECOV_TOKEN` is forwarded.
- Declare `CODECOV_TOKEN` in `checks.yml`'s `workflow_call.secrets` block (required to accept inherited secrets).
- Switch `fail_ci_if_error: true` and add `verbose: true` so future upload failures surface as CI failures rather than being silently swallowed.

## Diagnosis evidence (from CI logs)

```
-> Token of length 0 detected
error - Upload failed: {"message":"Token required because branch is protected"}
```

## Test plan

- [ ] CI runs green on this PR
- [ ] After merge, the next push to `main` should show coverage data in Codecov and the badge should change from "unknown" to a percentage

mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- The codecov badge was showing "unknown" because the `CODECOV_TOKEN` secret was missing from the `codecov/codecov-action` upload step
- Without the token, Codecov rejects uploads silently (the step has `fail_ci_if_error: false`, so CI stayed green)

## Root cause

The coverage job in `.github/workflows/checks.yml` generates `lcov.info` correctly via `cargo-llvm-cov`, but the subsequent upload step lacked authentication. Codecov v5+ requires a token for private repos and strongly recommends it for public repos to associate uploads correctly.

mergedPRs.title: feat(repl): add Lua-based custom meta commands
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Adds a Lua 5.4 runtime (via `mlua` crate, feature-gated as `--features lua`) so users can write custom backslash commands in `~/.config/rpg/commands/*.lua`
- New `\commands` meta-command lists all loaded custom Lua commands with descriptions
- Unknown backslash commands are matched against the registry before the "invalid command" error fires, so `\my_cmd arg1 arg2` Just Works once a script is installed
- Lua API: `rpg.register_command`, `rpg.query` (read-only tx), `rpg.print`, `rpg.pager`, `rpg.version`, `rpg.dbname`
- Two example scripts in `examples/commands/`: `bloat_check.lua` and `db_info.lua`
- When the `lua` feature is not compiled in, the registry is always empty and execution returns a graceful error message

## Architecture

- `src/lua_commands.rs` — `LuaRegistry` struct, command discovery, `rpg` module installation, handler invocation
- `src/metacmd.rs` — added `MetaCmd::ListCustomCommands` variant + `\commands` parser
- `src/repl/mod.rs` — `lua_registry` field on `ReplSettings`, loaded at session start; dispatch in `dispatch_meta` for both `ListCustomCommands` and fallback in the `Unknown` branch
- `rpg.query()` uses a thread-local client pointer + `block_in_place` / `Handle::block_on` for safe sync execution inside an async context

## Test plan

- [ ] `cargo test` — all 1643 tests pass
- [ ] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` — clean
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` — clean
- [ ] Manual: place a `.lua` script in `~/.config/rpg/commands/`, run rpg, invoke `\commands` and the custom command
- [ ] Verify `\commands` prints "no custom commands" when the directory is empty/absent
- [ ] Verify rpg starts cleanly without `--features lua` (empty registry, no crash)

Closes #659

mergedPRs.title: fix(repl): auto-rollback stale transactions after /ask errors
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
Fixes #638

## Summary

- Add `internal_tx: bool` flag to `ReplSettings` to track whether an open transaction was started by the internal `/ask` machinery (vs. an explicit user `BEGIN`). Only internal transactions are eligible for auto-rollback, preventing silent rollback of user transactions.
- Add `auto_rollback_stale_tx` async helper that checks `internal_tx && (InTransaction | Failed)`, issues `ROLLBACK`, resets `TxState::Idle`, clears the flag, and prints `-- auto-rolled back stale transaction`.
- Call `auto_rollback_stale_tx` at the top of `handle_line` so every incoming REPL command cleans up stale state before executing.
- In `handle_ai_ask`: set `internal_tx = true` before wrapping and executing the read-only SQL; after the error-path `client.simple_query("rollback")`, explicitly reset `*tx = TxState::Idle`; clear `internal_tx = false` on both success and failure paths. Same fix applied to the `AskChoice::Edit` path.
- Add 4 unit tests for detection logic: stale `InTransaction`, stale `Failed`, no rollback for user `BEGIN`, no rollback when `Idle`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `cargo test` — all 1532 tests pass, 4 new unit tests added
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` — clean
- [ ] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` — clean
- [ ] Manual: trigger `/ask` query, hit Ctrl-C mid-query — next command shows `-- auto-rolled back stale transaction` and prompt returns to `mydb=>`
- [ ] Manual: explicit `BEGIN; select 1;` without `COMMIT` — next command does NOT auto-rollback (user transaction preserved)

## Summary

- Add `with_ctrl_c_cancel()` helper that wraps any `Result`-returning future in `tokio::select!` against `tokio::signal::ctrl_c()`, dropping the HTTP connection and returning a `CANCELLED` sentinel when the signal fires

- SQL query cancellation via PostgreSQL `CancelRequest` was already in place (existing cancel guard task in the REPL loop); this PR does not change that path

mergedPRs.title: fix(pager): guard against non-TTY stdin before starting pager
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Fixes "Failed to initialize input reader" when `\?` or other backslash commands trigger the built-in pager in non-interactive/piped mode
- Root cause: on macOS, crossterm's `mio`/`kqueue` event reader fails to register a newly-opened `/dev/tty` fd (`EVFILT_READ` returns `EINVAL`). This happens when stdin is a pipe and `tty_fd()` opens `/dev/tty` as a new fd instead of reusing the inherited stdin fd 0. The global `InternalEventReader` ends up with `source=None`, causing every subsequent `event::poll()` to return "Failed to initialize input reader".
- Fix: add an early stdin TTY check in `run_pager()` — return `Err(Unsupported)` immediately when stdin is not a terminal. All three call sites (`repl/execute.rs`, `dba.rs`, `describe.rs`) suppress the error print for `Unsupported` errors (expected in piped/non-interactive mode) and fall back to plain `print!`.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build` succeeds
- [x] `cargo test` — 1528 tests pass
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` passes
- [x] Verified no "pager error" in piped mode: `printf "\\\\?\n\\\\q\n" | rpg -d postgres 2>&1 | grep "pager error"` — empty
- [x] Verified help text still prints in piped mode: `printf "\\\\?\n\\\\q\n" | rpg -d postgres 2>&1 | grep "Backslash commands"` — found
- [x] Verified no "pager error" in PTY+piped-stdin mode: `script -q /dev/null sh -c 'printf "\\\\?\n\\\\q\n" | rpg -d postgres 2>&1' | grep "pager error"` — empty

Closes #632.

mergedPRs.title: fix(ai): always wrap /ask queries in read-only tx
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #633: write queries in yolo mode now go through `start transaction read only` wrapper, providing DB-level enforcement
- Removes the `if read_only { wrap } else { borrow }` conditional in both `AskChoice::Yes` and `AskChoice::Edit` branches — always wraps unconditionally
- Rollback-on-error no longer conditions on `read_only`; all wrapped queries need cleanup on failure
- Updates yolo-mode warning message to accurately describe that the DB will reject write queries
- Adds 2 integration tests: `ask_yolo_write_query_rejected_by_readonly_tx` (INSERT rejected) and `ask_yolo_ddl_rejected_by_readonly_tx` (CREATE TABLE rejected)

## Behavior change

| Mode | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Non-yolo, read-only query | wrapped in read-only tx | wrapped in read-only tx (unchanged) |
| Non-yolo, write query | refused (AskChoice::No) | refused (AskChoice::No) (unchanged) |
| Yolo, read-only query | wrapped in read-only tx | wrapped in read-only tx (unchanged) |
| **Yolo, write query** | **executed bare (no protection)** | **wrapped in read-only tx → PG rejects** |
| Edit path, any query | wrapped only if read-only | always wrapped |

mergedPRs.title: fix(repl): route backslash command output through pager
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

Closes #625.

All backslash commands that can produce long output now route through the built-in pager when the content exceeds the terminal height, matching the behaviour of `\dba` commands (fixed in #622) and query results.

- **`\?`** — refactored to use the new shared `maybe_page` helper (eliminates duplicate inline logic)
- **`\h [topic]`** — SQL help list / synopsis now paged; introduced `sql_help_text()` returning `String`

Small outputs still skip the pager (controlled by `pager_min_lines`). `\o` file redirection continues to work correctly for all affected commands.

### Architecture

Added a `maybe_page(settings, text)` helper in `repl/mod.rs` that centralises the pager-or-print logic (terminal size check → statusline teardown → pager → statusline restore). The `\dba` commands have an equivalent helper in `dba.rs`; both follow the same pattern.

name: sqlever
description: Sqitch-compatible PostgreSQL migration tool with static analysis and zero-downtime migrations

mergedPRs.title: S5-3: Analysis rules SA011-SA021 — DML, DDL, and locking safety rules
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

Implements the remaining 11 static analysis rules from SPEC section 5.1, completing the full SA001-SA021 rule set:

- **SA011** (connected, warn): UPDATE/DELETE on large table — flags DML on tables with high estimated row counts when a DB connection is available
- **SA012** (static, info): ALTER SEQUENCE RESTART — informational flag for sequence restarts that may break ID assumptions
- **SA013** (static, warn): Missing SET lock_timeout before risky DDL — detects ALTER TABLE, CREATE INDEX, DROP TABLE, TRUNCATE, CLUSTER, VACUUM FULL, and REINDEX without a preceding lock_timeout guard
- **SA014** (static, warn): VACUUM FULL or CLUSTER — flags operations that take AccessExclusiveLock and rewrite the table
- **SA015** (static, warn): ALTER TABLE RENAME (table or column) — flags renames that break running application code
- **SA016** (static, error): ADD CONSTRAINT CHECK without NOT VALID — blocks deploy for CHECK constraints that scan under heavy lock
- **SA017** (hybrid, warn): SET NOT NULL on existing column — flags full table scan risk, with connected suppression stub
- **SA018** (hybrid, warn): ADD PRIMARY KEY without USING INDEX — flags extended AccessExclusiveLock during implicit index creation
- **SA019** (static, warn): REINDEX without CONCURRENTLY — flags AccessExclusiveLock on reindex
- **SA020** (static, error): CONCURRENTLY operations in transactional context — blocks deploy for operations that cannot run in transactions
- **SA021** (static, warn): Explicit LOCK TABLE — flags dangerous explicit locking in migrations

## Details

- All rules follow the exact same patterns as SA001-SA010: standalone exported function, AnalysisContext input, Finding[] output
- Registry updated from 11 to 22 rules in `src/analysis/rules/index.ts`
- 174 new tests in `tests/unit/analysis-rules-11-21.test.ts`
- 55 no-trigger fixture files (5 per rule) + 28 trigger fixtures across `tests/fixtures/analysis/SA0{11-21}/`
- Updated existing test to expect 22 rules in registry
- All 348 tests pass across both test files

## Test plan

- [x] `bun test tests/unit/analysis-rules-11-21.test.ts` — 174 pass, 0 fail
- [x] `bun test tests/unit/analysis-rules.test.ts` — 174 pass, 0 fail (existing tests still pass)
- [x] Each rule has trigger fixtures that produce findings and no-trigger fixtures that produce zero findings

Closes #52

  9. **Verify script failure** — change recorded in tracking tables (already committed), error reported

mergedPRs.title: S3-2: Deploy command
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Implement `sqlever deploy` -- the core command that executes pending migration scripts against PostgreSQL and tracks state in sqitch.* registry tables
- Full deploy flow per SPEC Section 7: parse plan, connect, acquire advisory lock, create registry, compute pending changes (topological sort), execute scripts via psql, record tracking, release lock
- Advisory lock coordination using two-arg `pg_try_advisory_lock(namespace, project_hash)` with application-computed stable hash (not hashtext) per DD13
- Non-transactional change support (`-- sqlever:no-transaction` comment detection), lock timeout guard integration (SPEC 5.9), signal handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM cleanup)
- Flags: `--to`, `--mode change|all|tag`, `--dry-run`, `--verify`, `--set key=value`, `--db-uri`, `--target`, `--lock-timeout`, `--db-client`
- Exit codes: 0 success, 1 failure, 4 concurrent deploy, 5 lock timeout, 10 DB unreachable
- Adds `Registry.recordFail()` for recording deploy failure events in sqitch.events
- 47 new unit tests with mocked DB and psql; all 747 tests pass

mergedPRs.title: S5-2: Analysis rules SA001-SA010 — DDL safety rules
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Implement 11 analysis rules (SA001-SA010) per SPEC Section 5.1, one file per rule in `src/analysis/rules/`
- Add shared types (`Rule`, `Finding`, `AnalysisContext`, `offsetToLocation`) in `src/analysis/types.ts`
- Add rule registry with `allRules` array and `getRule()` lookup in `src/analysis/rules/index.ts`
- 89 SQL fixture files across 11 rule directories in `tests/fixtures/analysis/SA00X/{trigger,no_trigger}/`
- 174 tests covering trigger cases, no-trigger cases, edge cases, PL/pgSQL body exclusion, version-aware behavior, and message/location quality

### Rules implemented

| Rule | Severity | Type | Trigger |
|------|----------|------|---------|
| SA001 | error | static | ADD COLUMN NOT NULL without DEFAULT |
| SA002 | error | static | ADD COLUMN DEFAULT volatile (any PG) |
| SA002b | warn | static | ADD COLUMN DEFAULT non-volatile (PG < 11) |
| SA003 | error | static | ALTER COLUMN TYPE (unsafe cast / USING) |
| SA004 | warn | static | CREATE INDEX without CONCURRENTLY |
| SA005 | warn | static | DROP INDEX without CONCURRENTLY |
| SA006 | warn | static | DROP COLUMN |
| SA007 | error | static | DROP TABLE (non-revert context) |
| SA008 | warn | static | TRUNCATE |
| SA009 | warn | hybrid | ADD FOREIGN KEY without NOT VALID |
| SA010 | warn | static | UPDATE/DELETE without WHERE |

### Design notes

- Each rule is a standalone function that takes a parsed AST and returns `Finding[]`, ready for integration with the analysis engine (issue #50)
- Uses `libpg-query` WASM module for SQL parsing in tests
- SA002 detects volatile functions (`random()`, `gen_random_uuid()`, `clock_timestamp()`, `txid_current()`, etc.) recursively through type casts and expressions
- SA002b is version-aware: only fires when `pgVersion < 11`
- SA003 fires on all type changes in static mode (conservative); USING clause always triggers
- SA007 respects `isRevertContext` flag for sqitch revert script exemption
- SA008/SA010 implement PL/pgSQL body exclusion per spec
- SA009 is typed as hybrid with static NOT VALID check implemented; connected index check is stubbed for DB integration

## Test plan

- [x] 174 tests pass (`bun test tests/unit/analysis-rules.test.ts`)
- [x] Full suite passes (875 tests, 0 failures)
- [x] Zero new TypeScript errors in source files
- [x] Each rule has >= 5 no-trigger fixtures
- [x] Trigger fixtures verify ruleId, severity, and message content
- [x] Cross-cutting tests verify location, field completeness, and edge cases

Closes #51

mergedPRs.title: fix: revert --to validation + advisory lock leak
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary
- **Bug 1 (`--to` without value):** `revert --to` with no argument silently fell through to reverting ALL deployed changes. Now validates that `--to` has a non-empty, non-flag value and throws a clear error (`Missing value for --to. Usage: revert --to <change>`).
- **Bug 2 (advisory lock leak):** All `process.exit()` calls inside `runRevert`'s try block bypassed the finally block, leaking the PostgreSQL advisory lock. Replaced every `process.exit()` in `runRevert` with `return <exitCode>` (changed return type from `Promise<void>` to `Promise<number>`). The caller in `cli.ts` now calls `process.exit()` after `runRevert` resolves, so the finally block always runs and releases the lock.
- Added 5 new tests covering both bugs: `--to` with missing value, `--to` followed by a flag, source-level assertion that `process.exit` is absent from `revert.ts`, and runtime verification that `runRevert` returns a numeric exit code.

## Test plan
- [x] All 39 revert unit tests pass (including 5 new ones)
- [x] Full test suite: 982 pass, 2 pre-existing integration failures (DB connectivity, unrelated)
- [ ] Manual: `sqlever revert --to` (no value) should print error, not revert everything
- [ ] Manual: kill a revert mid-flight and verify advisory lock is released

Closes #66

mergedPRs.title: S5-1: Analysis engine — analyzer, rule registry, preprocessor, suppression
mergedPRs.author: NikolayS
mergedPRs.body:
## Summary

- Implements the analysis engine foundation per SPEC section 5.1 (issue #50)
- **`src/analysis/types.ts`** — `Rule`, `AnalysisContext`, `Finding`, `Severity` interfaces matching the spec's rule interface contract
- **`src/analysis/registry.ts`** — Rule registry with duplicate ID validation and bulk registration
- **`src/analysis/preprocessor.ts`** — Strips psql metacommands (`\i`, `\ir`, `\set`, `\echo`, `\if`/`\endif`, `\!`, etc.) before parsing, replacing with same-length blank lines to preserve byte offsets and line numbers
- **`src/analysis/suppression.ts`** — Parses `-- sqlever:disable SA001` / `-- sqlever:enable SA001` inline suppression comments with block form, single-line trailing form, comma-separated rule IDs, unknown rule warnings, unclosed block warnings, unused suppression warnings, and `all` keyword rejection
- **`src/analysis/index.ts`** — `Analyzer` class with `analyze(filePath, options)` and `analyzeSql()` entry points: loads rules from registry, parses SQL via libpg-query, runs applicable rules (skipping connected rules without db), filters through suppressions and per-file overrides, applies severity overrides and `errorOnWarn` promotion, handles parse errors and rule exceptions gracefully

80 tests across 5 test files covering types, registry, preprocessor, suppression, and full analyzer pipeline integration.

Closes #50
